description_long: This object is a heavily weathered, rustic kettle made primarily of dark, aged wood, with visible cracks, chips, and surface wear. The body is cylindrical and slightly tapered, with a roughly cut opening at the top and a side-mounted handle that appears to be carved from the same material. The spout is short, stubby, and integrated into the body, showing signs of chipping and irregularity. The surface is marked with faded, possibly engraved or painted, alphanumeric characters, adding a utilitarian or archival feel. The base is uneven and roughly finished, suggesting handcrafting or significant age. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no visible lid and minimal decorative elements beyond the markings.
description: A rustic, weathered wooden kettle with a side handle, short spout, and faded alphanumeric markings. The surface is rough and aged, with no visible lid and a hand-carved appearance.
description_view_0: The top opening and interior are visible, showing rough, splintered wood and the side handle attachment.
description_view_1: The side view highlights the handle and the short, blocky spout, as well as the faded markings on the body.
description_view_2: The bottom of the kettle is visible, showing an uneven, chipped base and the dark, hollow interior.
description_view_3: The rear side is shown, with the handle and spout less visible, emphasizing the worn, plain wooden surface.
